title: How do you pronounce the Angels names
display_title: How do you pronounce the Angels names
wikitext:
[]: How do you pronounce the Angels' names?             Quote Post by TokioPunk483 » Sun Jul 10, 2011 12:01 am Obviously I can pronounce Adam, Lilith, and Lilim, but what about the other angels? I know how to pronounce Sachiel, Shamshel, and Tabris, but I have to guess on all the other names... Help?

[]:             Quote Post by symbv » Sun Jul 10, 2011 3:13 am You follow the original pronunciation as spoken in the anime: 第1使徒 アダム A-DA-MU (ADAM) 第2使徒 リリス LI-LI-SU (LILITH) 第3使徒 サキエル SA-KI-E-RU (SACHIEL) 第4使徒 シャムシエル SHA-MU-SHI-E-RU (SHAMSHEL) 第5使徒 ラミエル RA-MI-E-RU (RAMIEL) 第6使徒 ガギエル GA-GI-E-RU (GAGHIEL) 第7使徒 イスラフェル I-SU-RA-FE-RU (ISRAFEL) 第8使徒 サンダルフォン SA-N-DA-RU-FO-N (SANDALPHON) 第9使徒 マトリエル MA-TO-RI-E-RU (MATRIEL) 第10使徒 サハクィエル SA-HA-KUI-E-RU (SAHAQUIEL) 第11使徒 イロウル I-RO-U-RU (IREUL) 第12使徒 レリエル RE-RI-E-RU (LELIEL) 第13使徒 バルディエル BA-RU-DI-E-RU (BARDIEL) 第14使徒 ゼルエル ZE-RU-E-RU (ZERUEL) 第15使徒 アラエル A-RA-E-RU (ARAEL) 第16使徒 アルミサエル A-RU-MI-SA-E-RU (ALMISAEL) 第17使徒 タブリス TA-BU-RI-SU (TABRIS) 第18使徒 リリン RI-RI-N (LILIN)

[]:             Quote Post by Hyper Shinchan » Sun Jul 10, 2011 3:58 am I think that she's asking for an English-friendly transcription (you know, the "karaoki" issue mentioned in the other thread, in English "E" is pronounced as "E" and "I" at the same time, "A" is pronounced as "A" and "E" and so on). Ah, usually the 16th Angel is romanised and pronounced as Armisael, not Almisael. @TokioPunk483: you can hear the pronunciations of the Angels until Iruel in episode 14, for the others I can't really help.

[]:             Quote Post by ZapX » Sun Jul 10, 2011 4:22 am Easiest way I can tell you for English pronunciation is: Sock-ee-el Shawm-shell Rah-me-el Gah-gee-el Is-rah-fell Sawn-doll-fawn Mah-tree-el Sah-ha-kwee-el Ee-roo-el Leh-lee-el Bar-dee-el Zeh-roo-el Are-ah-el Are-mee-sah-el Tah-bris

[]:             Quote Post by symbv » Sun Jul 10, 2011 5:01 am Hyper Shinchan wrote: I think that she's asking for an English-friendly transcription (you know, the "karaoki" issue mentioned in the other thread, in English "E" is pronounced as "E" and "I" at the same time, "A" is pronounced as "A" and "E" and so on).

[]:             Quote Post by Hyper Shinchan » Sun Jul 10, 2011 5:07 am symbv wrote: I could tell English-friendly transcription is what she may want, but as the question is "How do you pronounce the Angels' names?" so I offered the authoritative answer -- we should follow how the characters pronounced their names

[]:             Quote Post by symbv » Sun Jul 10, 2011 8:20 am ^ I can't remember very clearly I must say.. Perhaps I should have said how they "could have been pronounced" by the characters?

[]:             Quote Post by Stryker » Sun Jul 10, 2011 9:48 am ZapX wrote: Sock-ee-el

[]:             Quote Post by Tankred » Sun Jul 10, 2011 10:01 am Stryker wrote: Really? In English context (at least, if it were spelt how it was sound), wouldn't it be Sa-chey-el?

[]:             Quote Post by Stryker » Sun Jul 10, 2011 10:04 am Alright.

[]:             Quote Post by Bagheera » Sun Jul 10, 2011 11:43 am symbv wrote: I could tell English-friendly transcription is what she may want, but as the question is "How do you pronounce the Angels' names?" so I offered the authoritative answer -- we should follow how the characters pronounced their names

[]:             Quote Post by Trajan » Sun Jul 10, 2011 1:40 pm I feel much better about myself knowing I've been pronouncing almost all of these right.

[]:             Quote Post by TehDonutKing » Sun Jul 10, 2011 3:31 pm Th is pronounced soft. Ch is pronounced the German way. Adam = aw-dawm Lilith = Don't know. I think "lu-luth". Sachiel = I WOULD know if SOME WIKI had friggin' DIACRITICS. Shamshel = Dunno. It's Aramaic. The Greek pronunciation is "sawm-seel". Ramiel = Again, no diacritics. NOTE TO INTERNET: VOWELS ARE IMPORTANT. The Greek pronunciation is "Raw-meel" Gagiel = I can't find the Hebrew spelling. What the hell. Israfel is Arabic. Sorry. Sandalphon = NO FUCKING DIACRITICS. Greek is "sawn-thawl-fone". (Greek th pronounced hard) Matarael. No idea. Sahaquiel = No diacritics. Ireul = No effin clue, because it isn't on wikipedia, and our wiki lacks diacritics. Leliel = lay-lih-ayl Bardiel = Not on wikipedia, our wiki lcks dcrtcs. Zeruel = Not on wikipedia, our wiki lcks dcrtcs. Arael = Not on wikipedia, our wiki lcks dcrtcs. Armisael = Not on wikipedia, our wiki lcks dcrtcs. Tabris = It's Arabic. No idea. Lilin = Bth wks lck dcrtcs. Gddmmt.

[]:             Quote Post by gatotsu911 » Sun Jul 10, 2011 3:42 pm See, this is where having three years of Hebrew education under my belt finally pays off. ("khi" = hard "ch", a sound which does not exist in ordinary English. If you've ever heard someone speaking Hebrew, Yiddish, German or Russian, among other languages, you've probably heard it. Or if you've ever heard someone pronounce "Chanukkah".) Adam = "ah-DAHM" Lilith = "lee-LEET" Sachiel = "sa-khi-EL" Shamshel = "shahm-SHEL" Ramiel = "rah-mee-EL" Gaghiel = "gah-gee-EL" Israfel = "ees-rah-FEL" Sandalphon = "sahn-dahl-PHOAN" Matarael = "mah-tah-rah-EL" Sahaquiel = "sah-hah-kee-EL" Iruel = "ee-roo-EL" Leliel = "leh-lee-EL" Bardiel = "bahr-dee-EL" Zeruel = "zeh-roo-EL" Arael = "ah-rah-EL" Armisael = "ahr-mee-sah-EL" Tabris = "tah-BRIS" (or maybe "tah-VRIS", I'm not entirely sure) Lilin = "lee-LEEM" (yes, that's an "m") And in case you were curious, "el" = "god", hence most angels (and some people - myself included) have it as part of their name.

[]:             Quote Post by TehDonutKing » Sun Jul 10, 2011 3:50 pm THANK YOU. The only experience I have is a guide in the back of a Bible.

[]:             Quote Post by Viking » Sun Jul 10, 2011 4:42 pm gatotsu911 wrote: [..] And in case you were curious, "el" = "god", hence most angels (and some people - myself included) have it as part of their name.

[]:             Quote Post by gatotsu911 » Sun Jul 10, 2011 4:48 pm Viking wrote: As well as Superman and his relatives

[]:             Quote Post by BornIn1142 » Sun Jul 10, 2011 5:33 pm Viking wrote: As well as Superman and his relatives

[]:             Quote Post by TokioPunk483 » Sun Jul 10, 2011 5:38 pm gatotsu911 wrote: Adam = "ah-DAHM" Lilith = "lee-LEET" Sachiel = "sa-khi-EL" Shamshel = "shahm-SHEL" Ramiel = "rah-mee-EL" Gaghiel = "gah-gee-EL" Israfel = "ees-rah-FEL" Sandalphon = "sahn-dahl-PHOAN" Matarael = "mah-tah-rah-EL" Sahaquiel = "sah-hah-kee-EL" Iruel = "ee-roo-EL" Leliel = "leh-lee-EL" Bardiel = "bahr-dee-EL" Zeruel = "zeh-roo-EL" Arael = "ah-rah-EL" Armisael = "ahr-mee-sah-EL" Tabris = "tah-BRIS" (or maybe "tah-VRIS", I'm not entirely sure) Lilin = "lee-LEEM" (yes, that's an "m")

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Sun Jul 10, 2011 5:53 pm TokioPunk483 wrote: First, Sandalphon. How do you pronounce the "phon" part? I'm a little confused.
